title: Deaths due to unsafe sex
subtitle: Estimated annual number of deaths attributed to [unsafe sex](#dod:unsafe_sex). Unsafe sex is defined as the risk of disease due to sexual transmission. Unsafe sex is an important risk factor in cervical cancer, HIV and sexually-transmitted infections.
caption:
Deaths due to unsafe sex, 2023

Estimated annual number of deaths attributed to unsafe sex. Unsafe sex is defined as the risk of disease due to sexual transmission. Unsafe sex is an important risk factor in cervical cancer, HIV and sexually-transmitted infections.

The choropleth map displays the estimated annual number of deaths attributed to unsafe sex across countries in 2023, using a color scale that ranges from light beige for lower counts to deep maroon for the highest values, with specific bands marking thresholds at 1,000, 3,000, 10,000, 30,000, 100,000, and 300,000 deaths. The visualization highlights a concentration of the highest mortality figures in southern Africa and parts of South Asia. South Africa appears in the darkest maroon shade, placing it in the highest category of over 300,000 deaths. India and China are shaded in a deep red tone, indicating values falling within the 100,000 to 300,000 range. Moving to the next tier, countries such as the United States, Brazil, Russia, and Mexico are colored in a medium orange, corresponding to the 10,000 to 30,000 band. Much of Western Europe, Canada, and Australia appear in the lightest beige shades, signifying death counts below 1,000. Greenland and several nations in central Africa and the Middle East are marked with diagonal hatching to denote that no data is available for those regions. The metric is age-standardized to allow comparisons between countries and over time, and not all deaths in a country may have been registered with a cause of death. The geographic distribution shows the most intense coloring clustered in the southern tip of the African continent and the Indian subcontinent, while the Americas and Europe generally display lighter shades indicating lower absolute numbers of deaths.
